Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 211. (no title) (CT 53 494)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

[x x x] ⸢x x x⸣ [x x x x]

o 2'2'

[x x] ⸢lid⸣ : ina URU.ḫi-⸢in⸣-[da-na]

(2) [......] in Hin[dana]

o 3'3'

[x x] ⸢ta⸣-šam-a-ni-šú [x x x]

(3) [......] you listen to him [...]

o 4'4'

[x x] ⸢ša⸣ TAv ŠÀ-bi URU.[x x x]

(4) [......] from the city of [...]

o 5'5'

[x x x] URU.ḫi-in-⸢da⸣-[na x x]

(5) [......] Hinda[na ...]

o 6'6'

[x x x x] me [x x x x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P313907/.
